title: An Abelian (1840051,2350,3)-Difference Set
lean_gate_reason: Lean would only seal the reduction lemma. The packet still lacks a final existence or nonexistence theorem and is not close enough to paper-ready for formalization to change the publication verdict.
micro_paper_assessment: Fail for the current packet. The strongest honest claim is a verified multiplier-orbit reduction, but it is not a title-theorem closure, and the remaining gap is substantive new mathematics rather than light packaging.
family_affinity: Strong residual abelian lambda=3 case with prime-order cyclic compression through the multiplier subgroup <2347> of order 47.
generalization_signal: Prime-order plus prime-multiplier orbit compression still looks reusable, but the decisive local-to-global obstruction is missing and the exact orbit-pair computation is not yet reproducible from the saved artifact.
strongest_honest_claim: If an abelian (1840051,2350,3)-difference set exists, then the ambient group is cyclic of prime order 1840051 and, after translation, the set is a union of exactly 50 cosets of the order-47 subgroup <2347> in F_1840051^*.
candidate_theorem_slice: Any abelian (1840051,2350,3)-difference set is cyclic and, after translation, is a union of exactly 50 cosets of the order-47 subgroup <2347> in F_1840051^*.
paper_title_hint: Multiplier-Orbit Reduction for the Abelian (1840051,2350,3) Difference-Set Case
next_action: Move this slug aside as a reduction-only partial. Reopen it only if a direct global obstruction on the 50-coset quotient model or an explicit construction becomes available; do not expand it into a broader theorem program.
